steps.title: Read the y-intercept from the graph
steps.content: The line crosses the y-axis at $(0,-1)$, so $b=-1$. All four choices already use $-1$ as the y-intercept, so the slope is what distinguishes them.

steps.title: Determine the slope's sign and magnitude
steps.content:
The line descends from upper left to lower right, so the slope is negative — eliminating any positive-slope option. Reading two clean lattice points on the graph, $(0,-1)$ and $(8,-6)$:
$$m = \frac{-6-(-1)}{8-0} = \frac{-5}{8} = -\frac{5}{8}$$
So $f(x) = -\frac{5}{8}x - 1$.

steps.title: Compare with the answer choices
steps.content:
• A. $f(x)=\frac{5}{8}x-1$ — positive slope, wrong direction; eliminated.
  • B. $f(x)=\frac{8}{5}x-1$ — positive slope; eliminated.
  • C. $f(x)=-\frac{5}{8}x-1$ — matches our derived equation.
  • D. $f(x)=-\frac{8}{5}x-1$ — correct sign but the line is shallower than 45 degrees, ruling out a magnitude greater than 1; eliminated.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
